evidence: He is the founder and Vice-President of Bayesian Logic, Inc., a data analysis start-up under contract with the UN to build a new Nuclear Test Ban Treaty global monitoring system.

source.name: Stuart Russell
source.notes_excerpt: Stuart Russell is a professor of computer science at the University of California, Berkeley and holds the Smith-Zadeh Chair in Engineering. He co-authored the textbook 'Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach' with Peter Norvig, which is used in over 1,500 universities in 135 countries. target.name: Mila - Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute

evidence: the Colorado AI Policy Work Group, made up of a diverse membership including groups representing consumers, hospitals, school districts, and other users of technology, and large and small technology companies, convened by Governor Polis

source.name: Jared Polis
source.notes_excerpt: Jared Polis is the 43rd Governor of Colorado, having assumed office in 2019. He previously served as the United States Representative from Colorado's 2nd congressional district from 2009 to 2019. Polis convened the Colorado AI Policy Work Group to develop a framework addressing the use of AI and Automated Decision Making Systems in consequential decisions about consumers. He is the first openly ga

target.name: Colorado AI Policy Work Group
target.notes_excerpt: Convened by Governor Jared Polis (Oct 2025) to revise Colorado AI Act (SB 24-205), the nation's first comprehensive AI anti-discrimination law. Diverse membership: consumer groups, hospitals, school districts, tech companies large and small. Delivered unanimous support for revised policy framework (March 2026) emphasizing transparency over bias audits: up-front notice when AI is used in consequent

evidence: Prior to joining Anthropic, Fish was already urging AI companies and researchers to seriously consider the issue. In 2024 he co-founded the AI welfare research organization Eleos AI

source.name: Kyle Fish
source.notes_excerpt: Kyle Fish is Anthropic's first in-house AI welfare researcher, hired to determine whether models like Claude can have conscious experiences and how the company should respond. He is trained in neuroscience and spent years in biotech, cofounding companies that used machine learning to design drugs and vaccines for pandemic preparedness. Fish estimates a 20% chance that today's large language models

target.name: Eleos AI
target.notes_excerpt: Nonprofit research org focused on AI welfare, consciousness, and moral patienthood. Co-founded by Kyle Fish, Robert Long, Kathleen Finlinson. Published landmark report "Taking AI Welfare Seriously." Commended Anthropic's model welfare efforts. Five research priorities: welfare interventions, human-AI cooperation, leveraging AI for welfare research, standardized evaluations, credible communication.

evidence: Jennifer Pahlka is a co-founder and board chair of the Recoding America Fund

source.name: Jennifer Pahlka
source.notes_excerpt: Jennifer Pahlka is the founder of Code for America and author of 'Recoding America: Why Government Is Failing in the Digital Age and How We Can Do Better'. She served as U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer under President Obama from 2013-2014, where she helped found the United States Digital Service. She is currently a senior fellow at the Niskanen Center and the Federation of American Scientists

target.name: Recoding America
target.notes_excerpt: Book by Jennifer Pahlka (2023) diagnosing why government technology systems fail and outlining how modernizing digital infrastructure is essential for effective public services and democratic accountability.

source.name: Paul Christiano

He founded the Alignment Research Center, a nonprofit research organization that seeks to align future machine learning systems with human interests. He previously ran the language model

target.name: Model Evaluation and Threat Research

Worked with OpenAI (o3, GPT-4.5) and Anthropic (Claude). Does NOT accept lab funding to avoid conflicts of interest. Budget ~$13-15M/year. Funded via Audacious Project (TED) and donations. evidence: She is a co-founder of Black in AI, an advocacy group that has pushed for more Black roles in AI development and research.

source.name: Timnit Gebru
source.notes_excerpt: Timnit Gebru is the founder and executive director of The Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research (DAIR) Institute. She was co-lead of the Ethical AI research team at Google until her firing in 2020 (along with her co-lead) for writing a paper warning about the dangers of large language models and raising issues of discrimination in the workplace. She co-founded Black in AI, a nonprofit that

target.name: Black in AI
target.notes_excerpt: Nonprofit founded 2017 by Timnit Gebru and Rediet Abebe. Increases presence and inclusion of Black people in AI. Four pillars: education pathways, financial barriers, visibility, and community support. Received MacArthur Foundation funding. Hosts annual workshop at NeurIPS. Aims to broaden voices in AI development, deployment, and regulation.

evidence: His other businesses include the neurotechnology company Neuralink, which he co-founded in 2016

source.name: Elon Musk
source.notes_excerpt: Elon Musk is a businessman and entrepreneur known for his leadership of Tesla, SpaceX, X, and xAI. Musk has been the wealthiest person in the world since 2025; as of April 2026, Forbes estimates his net worth to be US$809 billion. He made predictions at the World Economic Forum that artificial intelligence will be smarter than all of humanity by the end of the decade. Musk spent more than $290 mil

target.name: Neuralink
target.notes_excerpt: Brain-computer interface company co-founded by Elon Musk (2016). Developing implantable BCI (N1 chip) for paralysis patients. FDA IDE approval May 2023; first human implant Jan 2024 (PRIME trial). FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for speech restoration tech (May 2025). Raised over $700M. Regulatory gap: neural data privacy laws lag behind technology. Competitor Precision Neuroscience also recei

evidence: In 2019, Liang founded High-Flyer AI which was dedicated to research on AI algorithms and its basic applications

source.name: Liang Wenfeng
source.notes_excerpt: Liang Wenfeng is a Chinese entrepreneur born in 1985 who serves as CEO of DeepSeek and co-founder of quantitative hedge fund High-Flyer. He launched DeepSeek in 2023 with backing from High-Flyer, focusing on developing general artificial intelligence through efficient, low-cost approaches. DeepSeek gained international attention in January 2025 with the release of DeepSeek-R1, which achieved perfo

target.name: High-Flyer AI
target.notes_excerpt: High-Flyer (Zhejiang High-Flyer Asset Management) is a Chinese quantitative hedge fund founded by Liang Wenfeng. In 2023 it incubated DeepSeek as a spin-off AI lab, funding it internally. High-Flyer posted 56.6% average returns in 2025 and manages over 70 billion yuan (~$10B USD).

evidence: In 2017, she co-founded AI4ALL, a nonprofit organization working to increase diversity in the field of artificial intelligence

source.name: Fei-Fei Li
source.notes_excerpt: Fei-Fei Li is a Chinese-born American computer scientist best known for establishing ImageNet, the dataset that enabled rapid advances in computer vision in the 2010s. She is a professor of computer science at Stanford University, with research expertise in artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, computer vision, and cognitive neuroscience. She has been called the godmother of AI

target.name: AI4ALL
target.notes_excerpt: Nonprofit co-founded in 2017 by Fei-Fei Li, Olga Russakovsky, and Rick Sommer to increase diversity in AI. Grew from 2015 Stanford summer program for high school girls. 2022 strategic pivot to College Pathways — accelerating underrepresented college students into AI internships and research. Partnerships with 16+ universities (Texas A&M, UIUC, UMBC). Early funders include Melinda French Gates/Pivo

evidence: He also founded the Trust::Data Alliance, an alliance of companies and nations building open-source software that makes AI and data safe, trusted and secure

source.name: Sandy Pentland
source.notes_excerpt: Alex Paul "Sandy" Pentland (born 1951) is an American computer scientist, HAI Fellow at Stanford, Toshiba Professor at MIT, and serial entrepreneur. He helped create and direct the MIT Media Lab and the Media Lab Asia in India, and is a HAI Fellow at Stanford. Pentland is one of the most cited authors in computer science, with an h-index of 155. He is a member of the U.S. National Academy of Engin

target.name: Trust::Data Alliance
target.notes_excerpt: MIT-based consortium (Trust::Data Consortium) founded by Alex 'Sandy' Pentland. Builds open-source tools for secure, privacy-preserving data sharing using blockchain and AI. Mission: create trusted internet-based network for safe data exchange. Focus areas: data cooperatives, privacy-preserving identity systems, safe distributed computation. evidence: In 2010 he founded Jukedeck, one of the first AI music generation startups

source.name: Ed Newton-Rex
source.notes_excerpt: Ed Newton-Rex is the CEO of Fairly Trained, a non-profit he founded in 2024 that certifies generative AI companies for fairer training data practices. He previously founded Jukedeck in 2010, one of the first AI music generation startups, which was acquired by ByteDance in 2019. He was named in TIME Magazine's list of the 100 most influential people in artificial intelligence in 2025. He organized

target.name: Jukedeck
target.notes_excerpt: British AI music startup (founded 2012 by Ed Newton-Rex) that built a platform for generating royalty-free music using AI. Raised $2.5M from Cambridge Innovation Capital, Parkwalk Advisors, Backed VC, Playfair Capital. Acquired by ByteDance/TikTok in July 2019; site went offline post-acquisition. Technology integrated into ByteDance's AI music capabilities. Newton-Rex became director of ByteDance
